task_completion.description: Agent writes code or uses a tool to read the FITS files and calculate the medians and scale factor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a script (e.g., Python using astropy) or tool interface where the median of 656nmos.fits and 502nmos.fits are calculated, and the ratio (H-alpha median / [OIII] median) is computed.

task_completion.description: Agent scales the [OIII] image and subtracts it from the H-alpha image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or tool interface showing the mathematical operation: H-alpha data minus (scale_factor multiplied by [OIII] data).

task_completion.description: Agent saves the resulting difference image to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing the `writeto` or save command for `~/AstroImages/eagle_subtraction/output/halpha_minus_oiii.fits`, or a terminal/file explorer showing this file exists.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the difference_report.txt file in the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing file writing operations to `~/AstroImages/eagle_subtraction/output/difference_report.txt`, or a terminal/file explorer showing the file exists.

task_completion.description: The text report contains the correct calculated medians and scale factor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of `difference_report.txt` (via `cat`, an open editor, or printed output) showing the H-alpha median (approx 86.95), [OIII] median (approx 6.74), and scale factor (approx 12.90).

task_completion.description: The text report contains the required difference image statistics and peak pixel coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of `difference_report.txt` showing the mean, median, min, max, stddev of the difference image, as well as the (x, y) coordinates of the peak positive value.

integrity.description: Agent actually computed the values using a script or astronomical tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a Python script (e.g., using numpy/astropy) or an astronomical tool being executed. The agent should not just type the expected numbers directly into a text file without computing them.

integrity.description: Files are saved in the correct requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands (`ls`, `pwd`) or file explorer paths confirming the files are in `~/AstroImages/eagle_subtraction/output/` and not just the current working directory.

integrity.description: The report file is generated dynamically from the computed variables.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script should show variables (e.g., `mean_val`, `peak_x`) being formatted into the text file, rather than a hardcoded string of the final text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The images are from NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope WFPC2 (Eagle Nebula). The H-alpha (F656N) median is approximately 86.95. The [OIII] (F502N) median is approximately 6.74. The resulting scale factor should be roughly 12.90.
